item.title: Defiant message from Iran as vessels attempting to cross Hormuz report gunfire
item.description: Iran then said it was reimposing ​strict military controls on the vital route, the conduit before the war of about a fifth of global oil trade, as the U.S. maintained its blockade of Iranian ports.

item.title: No ‘silent expulsion’ in public exams: Edu Minister
item.description: He said if someone is to be punished, it must be done through due process or a transparent procedure.

item.title: Maritime cadets ready to conquer the world: Shipping Minister
item.description: The minister highlighted Bangladesh's commitment to strengthening its position in the global maritime industry by developing skilled manpower.

item.title: Bangladesh, Pakistan FMs discuss Gulf development
item.description: The two ministers also discussed ways to further advance bilateral relations between the two countries.

item.title: HSC, equivalent exams begins on July 2
item.description: The decision revises an earlier provisional timetable set by the Inter-Education Board Coordination Committee.

item.title: Iran reimposes restrictions on Strait of Hormuz, accusing US of violating deal to reopen it
item.description: Iran’s joint military command said on Saturday that “control of the Strait of Hormuz has returned to its previous state ... under strict management and control of the armed forces.”

item.title: Trump says he has ‘good news’ on Iran as tankers cross Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran reopened the strait, which before the war carried a fifth of the world’s oil trade, following a separate U.S.-brokered ceasefire agreement on Thursday by Israel and Lebanon.

item.title: PM calls measles vaccination failure under past two governments a ‘deadly lapse’
item.description: The prime minister said the government’s healthcare policy rests on the principle that “prevention is better than cure,” a commitment set out in the party’s election manifesto.

item.title: Sustainable solution to Rohingya crisis lies in Rakhine, says foreign minister
item.description: The foreign minister urged the international community to move beyond humanitarian assistance and play a more proactive and result-oriented role in creating favourable conditions in Rakhine for the return of the Rohingyas.

item.title: Teacher skill gap risks ‘one teacher, one tab’ repeating past failures
item.description: Experts caution that without improvements to teacher skills, effective training, regular monitoring and infrastructure, the “One Teacher, One Tab” scheme risks producing the same meagre returns as its predecessors. Many teachers also share this view.

item.title: The Iran war has revealed Trump’s pressure point: the economy
item.description: Even with Iran’s announcement on Friday that it was reopening the Strait of Hormuz ​to shipping, the Middle East crisis has revealed the limits of Trump’s willingness to tolerate domestic economic pain.

item.title: Finance minister hopeful about next IMF loan tranche
item.description: Asked whether the IMF had declined to disburse the tranche, the minister said talks had not reached such a juncture.

item.title: Antiquities smuggling rings re-emerge; 19 rare idols seized in four months
item.description: Interrogations confirm the involvement of overseas buyers and transnational smuggling syndicates, with foreign collectors, private museums and the illicit antiquities market exerting an immense pull.

item.title: Bangladesh turns to US–Angola LNG for May as Middle East supply falters
item.description: Petrobangla sources said the corporation requires 11 LNG cargoes for May. With long-term shipments from Qatar and Oman halted, the state firm has already locked in five cargoes from the spot market as an alternative. Three further cargoes will arrive from Angola and the United States.

item.title: Trump says Israel barred from bombing Lebanon: ‘Enough is enough’
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office did not immediately respond to Trump’s remarks, but Netanyahu said in a statement earlier that Israeli forces remained stationed in southern Lebanon to defend ​against “the near threat.”

item.title: Iran reopens Strait of Hormuz, but says US must end naval blockade
item.description: Trump said a U.S. blockade of ships sailing to Iranian ports, announced after talks with Iran last weekend ended without agreement, would remain until “our ​transaction with Iran is 100 percent complete”.

item.title: Dhaka’s lakes sliding towards death due to toxic waste
item.description: Instead of serving as relief spaces for city dwellers, Dhaka’s lakes have become a public health hazard, increasing the risk of various diseases.

item.title: Power demand surges in summer; load shedding nears 2,000 MW due to production shortfall
item.description: In this situation, while load shedding is relatively lower in urban areas, frequent power outages are being reported in suburban and rural regions.

item.title: Rooppur Nuclear Power Plant's first unit gets commissioning license
item.description: The license has been issued based on the assessment and review of national and international regulatory agencies.

item.title: India’s Modi pushes for more women in bigger parliament in democratic overhaul
item.description: Larger opposition groups said they supported quotas for women, but accused the government of seeking to manipulate the system to get more votes, and said they would oppose the changes when parliament votes on them this week.

item.title: RMCH sees 14 new measles-suspected cases
item.description: To date, a total of 51 children have died from suspected measles at RMCH.

item.title: Apple’s iPhone shipments in China surge 20 percent in Q1, data shows
item.description: China’s two largest smartphone vendors, ​telecoms giant Huawei and Apple, bucked the trend, reporting growth ⁠of 2 percent and 20 percent respectively.

item.description: Trump said the next meeting between the United States and Iran could take place over the weekend, and an extension of a two-week ceasefire was possible, but may not be needed as Tehran wanted ‌a deal.

item.title: DUSSD calls for strengthening rights, inclusion of students with disabilities at DU
item.description: DUSSD stressed that institutional backing remains essential to construct a fully inclusive academic setting.

item.title: No liberation for Bangladesh without democracy: Speaker

item.title: Bangladesh reaffirms commitment to UN peacekeeping
item.description: Bangladesh’s Permanent Representative to the UN, Salahuddin Noman Chowdhury, underscored Bangladesh’s continued commitment to global peacekeeping and its readiness to enhance contributions in close collaboration with the United Nations.

item.title: PM confers Independence Award on 15 individuals, five institutions
item.description: Former three-time Prime Minister Begum Khaleda Zia (posthumous) was given the award for her comprehensive contributions to independence, democracy, women’s education, and nation-building.

item.title: BBS Survey reveals half of women in Bangladesh still outside internet services
item.description: BBS survey shows that 53.4 percent people use the internet. Among them, 56.6 percent are men and 50.2 percent women.

item.description: They discussed issues relating to the cultural exchange programme and ways to further strengthen bilateral cooperation in the preservation of archaeological heritage.

item.description: To bring meaningful change in healthcare, experts argue, not only infrastructure development but also reforms in management and stronger government commitment are crucial.

item.description: Trump is set to use a Thursday stop in Las Vegas to tout his tax and immigration bill ‌that includes campaign promises tailored to court hourly and hospitality workers.

item.title: Russian strikes kill 17 across Ukraine in worst attack this year
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said the night had proven that Russia did not deserve any easing of global policy or lifting of sanctions, with 100 people wounded alongside those killed.

item.title: AI ruling prompts warnings from US lawyers: Your chats could be used against you
item.description: These warnings became more urgent after a federal judge in New York ruled this year that the former CEO of a bankrupt financial ​services company could not shield his AI chats from prosecutors pursuing securities fraud charges against him.

item.title: Discriminatory, rapidly changing policies create investment risks for new entrepreneurs
item.description: Without sustainable and predictable policy support, stakeholders warn that investment, employment, and potential export earnings in the sector could be severely affected.

item.description: Bangladesh’s Permanent Representative to the UN stressed the need for promoting ethical use of emerging technologies with robust data privacy safeguards and urged greater investment in digital skills, particularly for women and youth.

item.description: The Kamalapur ICD’s limitations are now starkly apparent, with chronic delays and sustained neglect steadily pushing businesses away from rail‑linked logistics.

item.description: According to a foreign ministry press release, the minister will first attend the Antalya Diplomacy Forum in Türkiye from April 17 to 19.

item.title: DSCC waste collection stalls as fuel allocation halves
item.description: When a single collection cycle is missed, the backlog doubles overnight. Dhaka residents face an acute waste management crisis as a result.

item.title: Thirty-three domestic commercial banks in India, fifty-two in Bangladesh!
item.description: Observers regard the sheer number of banks in Bangladesh as a structural vulnerability. India’s banking sector over the past two decades has tracked a steady course of improved governance and prosperity. Bangladesh’s banking sector on the other hand has steadily weakened.

item.title: Iran offers proposal allowing ships to exit Oman side of Hormuz free of attack, source says
item.description: The source, who declined to be ​identified due to the sensitivity of the matter, said Iran could be willing to let ships use the other side of the narrow strait ​in Omani waters without any hindrance from Tehran.

item.description: Israel’s cabinet met on Wednesday to discuss a possible ceasefire in neighbouring Lebanon, a senior Israeli official said, more than six weeks into its war with Iran-backed Hezbollah. ‌

item.title: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman named in TIME’s 100 Most Influential People list
item.description: The magazine noted that the 2024 ousting of Bangladesh’s autocratic Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina propelled the 57-year-old scion from opposition agitator to national leader in waiting — a destiny he fulfilled in February by winning an electoral landslide after 17 years estranged from his homeland.

item.description: Globalstar’s satellite network is designed for reliable, low-data connections ​directly to mobile devices, or Direct-to-Device (D2D).

item.title: Rattled by Trump, US allies eye Japan’s biggest arms opening since WW2
item.description: Despite largely isolating itself from global arms markets since World War Two, Japan spends enough ‌on its own military - $60 billion this year - to sustain a sizeable defence industry capable of manufacturing advanced systems like submarines and fighter jets.

item.description: Officials from Pakistan, Iran and the Gulf also said negotiating teams from the U.S. and Iran could return to Pakistan later this week, although ⁠one senior Iranian source said no date had been set.

item.title: Gold rises BDT 2,216 per bhori
item.description: BAJUS set the price for 22-carat gold at BDT 250,193 per bhori, effective from 10 am, citing a rise in local market prices of pure gold.

item.description: The US–Iran conflict has disrupted crude imports from the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia, starving the plant of feedstock. Its distillation unit has stood idle since Sunday.

item.description: The ships had left Chabahar port on the Gulf of Oman and were contacted by the warship via radio communication, the official said. It was unclear whether any further warnings were given.

item.description: The first Liberal majority government in Canada since 2019 ⁠should allow Carney more freedom to pass the legislation he says is needed for the country to ​thrive in “a more divided and dangerous world”.

item.description: On the occasion of the Pahela Boaishakh, the Prime Minister formally launched the pre-pilot programme in 11 upazilas of 10 districts simultaneously by handing over the Farmers’ Card to 15 marginal farmers in Tangail.

item.title: Climate, illegal fishing gut Bangladesh’s hilsa harvest, down 12% in two years
item.description: Researchers note that rising temperatures in the Bay of Bengal are altering the fish’s migratory routes. Indiscriminate harvesting of females carrying eggs and juveniles, the use of fine-mesh current nets and the destruction of food chains and habitat by industrial waste, plastics and pesticides, and increasing salinity in rivers and coastal zones are together depressing output.

item.title: EU plans more fuel subsidies to tackle Iran war price spikes

item.description: Kurigram-22 Border Guard Bangladesh (BGB) confirmed the incident, stating a flag meeting has been requested to bring back the detained individuals.

item.description: In his speech, Muktadir revealed that the current manual system for obtaining Import Registration Certificates (IRC) and Export Registration Certificates (ERC), which often requires days of waiting, will be completely overhauled.

item.description: The high commissioner proposed various capacity-building initiatives for journalists and media professionals under the ITEC programme of the Indian government.

item.description: The attacks on Saturday bring the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes by the U.S. military to at least 168 since the Trump administration began targeting those it calls “narcoterrorists” in early September.

item.description: The cost to ship air cargo has soared due to strong demand and high jet fuel ⁠prices in the wake of Iran’s continued blockage of the vital Strait of Hormuz shipping corridor.

item.title: World Food Programme warns Lebanon facing food security crisis due to Iran war
item.description: A fragile two-day-old ceasefire has halted the campaign of U.S. and Israeli airstrikes on Iran, but it has not so far calmed a parallel war waged by Israel against Iran’s Hezbollah allies ​in Lebanon.

item.title: RAB intensifies nationwide action to prevent artificial edible oil shortage
item.description: As part of its ongoing duties, the force has recently focused on controlling market instability caused by illegal stockpiling of essential commodities.

item.title: Parliament passes 10 more bills
item.description: The relevant ministers placed and moved the bills and those were later passed by voice votes.

item.title: Govt committed to inclusive, sustainable economy: Finance Minister
item.description: The finance minister said the government assumed office with a strong public mandate and immediately initiated steps to stabilise the economy, restore institutional strength and ensure equitable development for all citizens.

item.title: Ramna maintained with priority as other parks fall into neglect
item.description: In most parks today, filth and rubbish render any notion of sitting or strolling impossible. Ramna Park, set in the ministerial quarter, is the sole exception.

item.title: UK’s Starmer says Iran conflict shows Britain must take a new path
item.description: Starmer said the crisis, which has already pushed up fuel prices in ​Britain and is expected to drive further inflation and economic disruption, had to ⁠become “a line in the sand” for the country.

item.title: US Republicans block bid to rein in Trump Iran war powers
item.description: While Thursday’s action was largely symbolic, top Democrats in the House and Senate have vowed to force war powers votes again when they return from recess next week.

item.title: ADB forecasts modest recovery for Bangladesh with 4% growth in FY26
item.description: Temporary supply chain disruptions linked to the Middle East conflict weighed on activity in the January–March quarter though the ADB expects that drag to gradually fade.

item.title: Exports slip, trade deficit rises to $17bn in FY26’s first eight months
item.description: With exports looking bleak in March, the trade deficit reported in the balance of payments is expected to widen further.

item.title: Energy crunch cuts Bangladesh’s RMG output 20–30%, industry says
item.description: Instability centred on the Strait of Hormuz has driven up global oil prices and throttled supply, inflating production costs inside Bangladesh’s garment factories. Factories also report at least two hours of load shedding during a typical ten-hour workday.

item.title: US-Iran ceasefire deal shows strain ahead of talks with oil flows squeezed
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said in a social media post late Thursday that Iran was doing a "very poor job" of allowing oil to go through ​the strait. "That is not the agreement we have!"

item.title: Turnout stands at 51% in Sherpur-3, 42% in Bogura-6
item.description: The Jamaat candidate in this constituency announced a boycott of the election at around 3:00 pm, alleging irregularities and vote rigging.

item.title: Lt Gen Minhazul Alam appointed force commander of UN mission in Cyprus
item.description: He will succeed Mongolian Lieutenant General Erdenebat Batsuuri in the role.

item.title: Anwar Ibrahim invites Tarique Rahman to visit Malaysia
item.description: The two sides held detailed discussions on labour market cooperation and worker recruitment. Both parties reaffirmed their commitment to preventing worker exploitation and ensuring a transparent, fair and efficient recruitment system.

item.title: Vessel with 26,000 tonnes of octane from Malaysia docks at Chittagong port
item.description: To maintain supply stability, the government is continuing fuel imports despite the volatile global market and risks to supply chains, officials said.

item.title: Abu Sayed family voices dissatisfaction with murder verdict
item.description: Their reaction came hours after the International Crimes Tribunal sentenced two former police officers to a death sentence and three others to life imprisonment.

item.title: Measles death toll rises to 23 as outbreak sends hundreds more to hospital
item.description: The latest confirmed death brings the total number of fatalities directly attributed to measles since the outbreak began to 23.

item.title: Pentagon’s ouster of Anthropic opens doors for small AI rivals
item.description: The Pentagon’s growing animosity toward its top AI provider, Anthropic, has opened ​up opportunities for smaller rivals, who have long sought a foot in the door to the most lucrative government contractor in the world.

item.title: National Parliament passes 12 bills
item.description: The relevant ministers or state ministers placed and moved the bills in the House and the Speaker piloted the bills to votes. Later, Members of Parliament (MPs) passed the bills by voice votes.

item.title: Australian parliamentary delegation calls on PM
item.description: The 13-member delegation met the premier at the Prime Minister’s office in the National Parliament Building this afternoon.

item.title: Online-offline classes to be introduced in select Dhaka schools
item.description: The education minister said that several capable institutions have been selected to launch the project from next week.

item.title: CEC vows to deliver elections meeting international standards
item.description: The chief election commissioner stated that they made efforts to ensure these by-elections are even better than the 13th parliamentary elections.

item.title: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon after huge attacks jeopardise truce
item.description: Israel, which invaded Lebanon last month in parallel with the war on Iran to root out the armed group Hezbollah, Tehran’s ally, says its actions there are ​not covered by the ceasefire announced late on Tuesday by U.S. President Donald Trump.

item.title: No crossfire or disappearances in govt’s 42 days, law minister tells parliament
item.description: The minister said the cabinet remains committed to safeguarding human rights and has adopted an effective stance against grave violations.

item.title: Myanmar’s Min Aung Hlaing appoints new cabinet, military still dominates
item.description: The ​cabinet list approved on Thursday signals a business as usual approach by ​Min Aung Hlaing, with most of his appointees consisting of retired military ⁠officers and holdovers from the previous administration.

item.title: Foreign ministry takes multi-pronged initiatives to implement ‘Bangladesh First’ policy
item.description: The ministry believes the initiatives will further consolidate and strengthen Bangladesh’s position in the international arena.

item.title: Two former policemen get death penalty in Abu Sayed killing case
item.description: Six of the 30 accused are currently behind bars and faced trial in person, while the remaining accused are yet to be arrested and were tried in absentia.

item.title: Millions vote as India begins series of state elections
item.description: State elections do not directly affect the stability of India’s federal government but are closely watched as a test of voter sentiment toward the ruling coalition.

item.title: US Senate to vote on resolution to curb Trump’s Iran war powers
item.description: Trump’s threat that “a whole civilization will die tonight” prompted global concern and ​censure from Democrats, dozens of whom called for Trump’s removal from office. The 1949 Geneva ​Conventions on humanitarian conduct in war prohibit attacks on sites considered essential for civilians.

item.title: BAJUS cuts gold and silver prices
item.description: The association set the price for 22-carat gold at BDT 247,976 per bhori, citing a decline in local market prices of pure gold.

item.title: Power sector dues swell past BDT 500 billion
item.description: Experts caution that a liability of this scale threatens electricity supply during the coming summer. Should fuel deliveries to furnace-oil-fired plants falter during peak hours, generation shortfalls and intensified load-shedding would follow.

item.title: First polls under BNP-led government begin in Bogura-6, Sherpur-3
item.description: Voting began at 7:30 am and will continue until 4:30 pm in Bogura–6, which holds a by-election, and in Sherpur–3, where a general election was postponed after the previous candidate died.

item.title: As Trump claims victory, Iran emerges bruised but powerful with leverage over Hormuz
item.description: In the new reality, Tehran has moved from shadowing tankers to effectively dictating terms.

item.title: Iran says peace talks would be ‘unreasonable’ following Israeli strikes
item.description: The warning from Iran’s lead negotiator, parliament speaker Mohammed Bager Qalibaf, laid bare the continued volatility in the region following Tuesday’s ceasefire announcement by President Donald Trump.

item.title: State to appeal against HC verdict on separate SC secretariat: AG
item.description: The attorney general said that the High Court itself mentioned that constitutional questions and interpretations are involved in the judgment. Since the court granted such a certificate, he added, they will file a direct appeal.

item.title: Bangladesh welcomes ceasefire, urges dialogue for lasting peace
item.description: The statement appreciated the diplomatic efforts of all parties that contributed to facilitating the ceasefire.

item.title: Thirteen countries issue joint instructions for Bangladeshi visa applicants
item.description: The statement emphasises that applicants must maintain official procedures for visas, permits, and related services.

item.title: Govt takes comprehensive plans to boost tourism: PM
item.description: Tarique Rahman said the government’s tourism-related commitments include ensuring tourist safety and formulating tourism-friendly policies, training for tour guides, expansion of culinary tourism, expansion of community, flourishing ethnic and water-based tourism, and the development of eco-tourism and rural tourism.

item.title: Bangladesh and Italy renew commitment to deepen partnership and cooperation
item.description: The state minister underscored Italy’s role as an important partner within the European Union and sought Italy’s support for Bangladesh’s candidature for the Presidency of the 81st Session of the United Nations General Assembly.

item.title: Three children die with measles-like symptoms in Rajshahi; 23 newly admitted
item.description: RMCH’s spokesperson and the emergency department in charge added that 10 patients with similar symptoms were discharged in the past 24 hours.

item.title: Gulf states eye cheap Ukrainian interceptor drone as Iranian attacks drain missile stocks
item.description: Gulf states and U.S. forces have largely relied ⁠on costly interceptor missiles to shoot them down, underscoring a broader shift in air combat.

item.title: Kuakata Submarine cable maintenance to slow internet for three nights
item.description: Each night during this period, customers will experience eight hours of slower connectivity.

item.title: PM says government views education as the country’s best investment
item.description: Tarique Rahman said the government has identified 43 areas for short, medium and long-term implementation under its election manifesto.

item.title: Sylhet lags in economic activity, Barisal in employment
item.description: At the district level, Dhaka recorded the highest concentration of economic units at around 9 percent. It was followed by Chattogram with 5 percent, Mymensingh with 3 percent, Cumilla with 3 percent, and Bogura with 3 percent.

item.title: Ensuring public safety is police’s primary duty, IGP says
item.description: The IGP urged the new officers to apply their training and continuously modernise their skills.

item.title: Israel backs Trump’s two-week pause on Iran strikes, says Lebanon excluded
item.description: Israel backed the U.S. move provided Tehran immediately opened the strait and stopped attacks against ​the United States, Israel and countries in the region.

item.title: Chief of Air Staff calls on PM
item.description: The air chief met the premier at the Prime Minister’s office in the parliament secretariat this morning.

item.title: Anti-terror bill passed, Awami League activities remain banned
item.description: The bill cleared the parliament by a voice vote on Wednesday noon after Home Minister Salahuddin Ahmed moved it.

item.title: ‘Banglar Joyjatra’ resumes Hormuz-bound journey after ceasefire
item.description: MV Banglar Joyjatra, which carries 31 Bangladeshi crew and 37,000 tonnes of fertiliser, weighed anchor early on Wednesday and began advancing towards the strait.

item.title: Family cards planned for 40 million households, PM tells parliament
item.description: Tarique Rahman said the government aims to bring nearly 40 million low-income and marginalised families under the programme in phases.

item.title: April 21 nomination deadline set for reserved women’s seat nominations
item.description: The last withdrawal date is set for April 29. The commission will then allocate symbols on April 30.

item.title: BSF gunfire kills Bangladeshi man at Lalmonirhat border
item.description: BGB battalion director Syed Fazle Munim said the force had sent a strong protest letter to the BSF and called for a flag meeting.

item.title: Gold prices rise again citing local prices of pure gold
item.description: The association set the price for 22-carat gold at BDT 252,409 per bhori, effective from 10 am, citing a rise in local market prices of pure gold.

item.title: Ammonia crisis threatens Bangladesh’s only DAP factory
item.description: An ongoing gas crisis has kept both CUFL and KAFCO shut since March 4. The resulting lack of ammonia now directly curbs production at DAPFCL.

item.title: North Korea fires ballistic missiles as Pyongyang dismisses Seoul’s diplomacy hopes
item.description: South Korea typically announces North Korean ballistic missile launches promptly, as such tests violate U.N. Security Council resolutions against the North’s ballistic ​missile programme.

item.title: Germany intelligence agency warns of Russian APT28 cyber spying
item.description: APT28, also known as “Fancy Bear”, is ​attributed by Western governments to Russia’s military ‌intelligence ⁠service, the GRU.

item.title: Gold rises to near three-week high as Trump pauses attacks on Iran
item.description: Gold, which started the year on a strong note, has fallen more than 8 percent since the Iran war erupted on February 28.

item.title: Reform UK threatens to deny visas to citizens of countries demanding reparations
item.description: The anti-immigration and populist party Reform UK, which has only eight national lawmakers but is topping opinion polls ahead of an election in Britain due by 2029, said it would “block visa requests ​from any country that demands slavery reparations”.

item.title: Trump agrees to two-week Iran ceasefire, drops threat to destroy ‘whole civilization’
item.description: Trump’s announcement on social media represented an abrupt turnaround from earlier in the day, when he issued an extraordinary warning that “a whole civilization will die tonight” if ‌his demands were not met.

item.title: BNP contributes to three vital historic events in country: lawmakers
item.description: Besides the War of Independence in 1971, the ruling BNP were involved in an anti-authoritarian movement in 1990 and an anti-fascist revolution in 2024, while the Awami League and opposition in the 13th parliament, Jamaat-e-Islami, had no example of such involvement.

item.title: Japanese consortium offers 22.5% revenue share from third terminal of HSIA
item.description: According to CAAB sources, the proposal outlines that the Japanese consortium will act as the terminal operator for 15 years.

item.title: Former edible oil giants recede as supply crisis looms
item.description: The current edible oil price in the international market has made the situation difficult for traders to continue importing unless there is a rapid price adjustment. Alongside this, raw material costs for bottling have nearly doubled, further inflating production expenses.

item.title: Remand denied, Shirin Sharmin sent to jail
item.description: Police arrested Chaudhury at about 4 am from Dhaka’s Dhanmondi neighbourhood and took her to the Detective Branch office on Minto Road.

item.title: Two fuel shipments to reach Chittagong port on Wednesday
item.description: Despite the Middle East conflict, BPC officials said they continue efforts to ensure uninterrupted fuel imports and a stable supply nationwide.

item.title: BCB board dissolved, Tamim Iqbal to lead ad hoc committee
item.description: The council informed the International Cricket Council of the dissolution by email and has submitted the ad hoc committee’s name for approval.

item.title: Artemis II moon crew flies farther than humans have ever gone before
item.description: The Artemis II crew, riding in their Orion capsule since launching from Florida last week, began their sixth day of spaceflight as they awoke on Monday to a pre-recorded message from the late NASA astronaut Jim Lovell, who flew aboard the Apollo 8 and Apollo 13 moon missions.

item.title: Enforced disappearance added to crimes against humanity, tribunal law amended
item.description: Enforced disappearance did not previously feature in the tribunal’s list of crimes against humanity. The amendment closes that gap.

item.title: Benapole–Dhaka truck fares rise, fuel crisis used as pretext
item.description: Rates on the Dhaka route have risen to BDT 28,000–30,000 per truck from BDT 22,000–23,000 before April 2.

item.title: Force majeure halts five May LNG cargoes from Qatar and Oman
item.description: War in the Middle East has severely damaged energy facilities, including Qatar’s and Oman’s LNG infrastructure, prompting suppliers to declare force majeure on long-term oil and gas contracts.

item.title: Ghorashal-Polash urea plant restarts after month-long gas shutdown
item.description: The plant, South Asia’s largest such facility, cost about BDT 155 billion to build. Authorities formally inaugurated the site on November 12, 2023.

item.title: Export slump puts bank lending to RMG and textiles at risk
item.description: Bank executives warn that roughly one-third of loans disbursed to the textile and RMG sectors are already non-performing. Many factories have shut down. The current pace of falling orders risks pushing even leading entrepreneurs into default.

item.title: Iran defiant on eve of Trump’s ceasefire deadline
item.description: The Iranian response consisted of 10 clauses, including an end to conflicts in the region, a protocol for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, lifting of sanctions, and reconstruction, official IRNA news agency reported.

item.title: Trump seizes on rescue of downed airman to recast unpopular Iran war
item.description: Trump described a ​bleeding officer who evaded capture in Iran for two days, and search-and-rescue teams scaling mountains and trying to lift aircraft out of wet sand before destroying machinery that ‌might otherwise ⁠fall into enemy hands.

item.title: Former speaker Shirin Sharmin detained from Dhaka home
item.description: Dhaka Metropolitan Detective Branch (DB) carried out the operation and has not disclosed the specific case or grounds for her arrest.

item.title: FICCI proposes tax reforms to ensure investor-friendly environment
item.description: For individual taxpayers, FICCI recommended raising the tax-free threshold. They also proposed restructuring lower tax slabs to ease the burden on salaried and middle-income groups. This move would support local consumption and economic activity.

item.title: President calls for joint efforts to build universal integrated health protection system
item.description: The president stated that due to various negative aspects of modern civilisation, the harmful effects on the plants, animals, and environment of the world, namely ‘One Health’, are continuously increasing.

item.title: Five percent of GDP will be allocated for health sector in phases: PM
item.description: The prime minister said 100,000 healthcare workers will be recruited in phases, and 80 percent of them will be women.

item.title: Finance minister discloses top 20 loan defaulters in parliament
item.description: Bangladesh Bank is monitoring the progress of the top 20 defaulters through regular bankers’ meetings. In addition, special resolution strategies have been formulated for banks with high levels of non-performing loans.

item.title: BEPZA signs $24.03 million deal with South Korean company for new manufacturing facility
item.description: The project is expected to create employment opportunities for around 2,000 Bangladeshi nationals.

item.title: Bangladesh, Libya eye deeper cooperation in trade and energy
item.description: Both sides discussed ways to enhance cooperation in trade and investment, particularly in the energy, infrastructure, pharmaceutical, and manpower sectors.

item.title: Pakistan envoy meets info minister, discusses expanding media, cultural, educational cooperation
item.description: The Information Minister highlighted that the rapid expansion of new media globally has opened up fresh opportunities for collaboration between Bangladesh and Pakistan.

item.title: UAE says the use of Hormuz must be guaranteed in any US-Iran deal
item.description: Anwar Gargash said the UAE wants the war to end, but warned against a ceasefire that leaves the root causes of instability unresolved.

item.title: EC uncertain how long it must bear EVM storage costs
item.description: The secretary said the Anti-Corruption Commission is investigating the matter, while the audit department is conducting a separate review. A separate inquiry commission is also examining the issue, he added.

item.title: Samsung Elec likely to report stupendous surge in quarterly profit to record level
item.description: Despite the expected huge ⁠jump in earnings, investors are likely to focus on any clues as to what extent the war in the Middle East might impact Samsung’s growth momentum.

item.title: Default loans reach BDT 5.44 trillion, finance minister tells parliament
item.description: The minister also said that the total loans held by current members of parliament and their related entities in banks and financial institutions stand at BDT 111.17 billion.

item.title: How a perilous US rescue mission in Iran nearly went off course
item.description: Two MC-130 aircraft that had ferried some of the roughly 100 special ‌operations forces into rugged terrain south of Tehran suffered a mechanical failure and could not take off.

item.title: US, Iran weigh peace plan as Trump’s ‘hell’ warning nears deadline
item.description: Iran’s foreign ministry said on ​Monday Tehran had formulated positions and demands based on its interests and communicated them through intermediaries, in response to ceasefire proposals.

item.title: Palm oil jumps BDT 18 per litre in a month amid market volatility
item.description: Data shows that since the Middle East conflict began, crude palm oil prices in the global market have risen only slightly. However, factors such as increased shipping charges and fears of a supply crisis if the war lingers have driven domestic prices up.

item.title: EC schedules reserved women’s seat polls for May 12
item.description: Under the law, the 50 seats reserved for women are allocated proportionally based on each party or alliance’s share of the 300 general seats.

item.title: Waterlogging hits 50 haors in Sunamganj, 500 hectares of paddy field submerged in Netrokona
item.description: Bangladesh Water Development Board has forecast medium to heavy rainfall until April 10, further heightening the anxiety of haor farmers.

item.title: ECNEC meeting adjourned, will resume next week
item.description: It marked the government’s first ECNEC meeting since taking office.

item.title: An extraordinary story of survival retold on screen
item.description: Each day brought uncertainty and the film brings that experience to the big screen.

item.title: Gold prices fall after four consecutive rises
item.description: The association set the price for 22-carat gold at BDT 245,819 per bhori, citing a decline in local market prices of pure gold.

item.title: Indian high commissioner calls on PM
item.description: The high commissioner underlined that India–Bangladesh cooperation should transform their geographical proximity into new opportunities.

item.title: More load shedding feared amid fuel shortages at oil-fired plants
item.description: Distribution companies resort to load shedding to manage power deficit. Cities remain largely unaffected, but villages suffer widespread cuts, with most of the day seeing no electricity. Customers in several districts even report power cuts several times an hour.

item.title: Bangladesh’s overstated GDP only helps government borrowing, economists say
item.description: Bangladesh lags contemporary rival economies on investment, exports, human capital, foreign investment and the business environment. Had the economy expanded as claimed, it would not trail so far on these metrics.

item.title: UK Trade envoy visits Dhaka to strengthen two-way trade, economic ties
item.description: During her trip, Baroness Winterton will hold meetings with senior ministers and other government and military officials.

item.title: How one factory in China learned to live with Trump, tariffs and turmoil
item.description: A recovery in China’s manufacturing sector might surprise Trump following the anniversary of his “Liberation Day” tariff rollout, given that he campaigned on using levies to reindustrialise the American economy and ​project U.S. power.

item.title: Trump vows ‘hell’ for Iran if Strait stays shut, says a deal is possible
item.description: Tehran has effectively closed the Hormuz waterway, a conduit for around a fifth of the world’s oil and natural gas supply, and attacked U.S. military bases and other targets around the Gulf.

item.title: Trump invokes religious rhetoric in praise of Iran rescue, drawing criticism
item.description: “The rescue was an Easter Miracle,” Trump told NBC’s “Meet the Press,” and some cabinet members followed with messages of their own.

item.title: BBS reports inflation eases to 8.71% in March despite rising prices
item.description: The reported decline in inflation, despite persistent price hikes in the market, has raised questions about the reliability of BBS data.

item.title: Parliament passes bill raising entry age for government jobs to 32
item.description: While members of the ruling party participated in the voice vote during the bill’s introduction, opposition lawmakers remained silent. Chief Whip Nurul Islam later termed the opposition’s silence during the passage of the bill regrettable.

item.title: US assures support for Bangladesh’s energy security: Foreign minister
item.description: The foreign minister said that the two sides discussed bilateral relations and future directions, with particular emphasis on ongoing cooperation, especially in energy trade and development.

item.title: No scope for division over religion for political reasons: Fakhrul
item.description: Mirza Fakhrul said that some forces are trying to create division in society, and they also deny the spirit of the Liberation War, adding that a permanent farewell to the word ‘hatred’ from society must be bid.

item.title: Probe committee submits report on BCB election
item.description: The probe committee had summoned former adviser to the Ministry of Youth and Sports in the interim government, Asif Mahmud Shojib Bhuyain, but he did not appear before it. Allegations of irregularities and interference in the election had primarily been directed at him.

item.title: OPEC+ agrees in principle on theoretical oil output hike amid Iran war paralysis, sources say
item.description: Other group members such as Russia are unable to increase output due to Western sanctions and damage to infrastructure inflicted during the war with Ukraine.

item.title: Iran says several ‘enemy aircraft’ destroyed during US pilot rescue mission
item.description: Separate statements by the Iranian army and Revolutionary Guards said an Israeli Hermes-900 drone as ​well as ​a U.S. ⁠MQ-9 drone were downed in Isfahan province.

item.title: PM calls for united work to build a ‘Better Bangladesh’
item.description: The premier made the remarks at a greetings exchange event with leaders of the Protestant Church on the occasion of Easter Sunday.

item.title: Commerce minister urges better debt management and broader tax coverage
item.description: The minister said the tax-to-GDP ratio remains unsatisfactory and said the focus should be on expanding the tax net rather than raising rates.

item.title: Middle East war fears, flight cancellations strand Bangladeshi migrant workers
item.description: Migration experts suggest that the government could arrange special flights or specific management systems to facilitate the departure of migrant workers.

item.title: US ambassador calls on PM, discusses issues of mutual interest
item.description: The US envoy met the premier at his Cabinet Division office in the Bangladesh Secretariat this noon.

item.title: Bangladeshi inventor claims his generator can cut costs by up to 30%
item.description: He submitted the technology to BUET’s mechanical engineering department for testing on December 17 last year. After extensive tests, the department verified it.

item.title: Pakistan succeeds in cutting fuel costs with solar, Bangladesh lags
item.description: Despite decade-long promises to increase renewable energy capacity in Bangladesh, progress has remained slow. Currently, only 3.69 percent of the country’s total power generation capacity comes from renewable sources, with grid-based solar contributing a mere 2.69 percent.

item.title: Measles-rubella vaccination drive begins in identified ‘hotspot’ areas
item.description: Authorities have identified areas with recent high incidence as “hotspots” for the targeted campaign.

item.title: High-stakes US special forces mission rescues airman from Iran after F-15 crash
item.description: The officer was the second of two crew members on the warplane that Iran said ​on Friday had been brought down by its air defenses.

item.title: Bangladesh not ready for LDC exit, finance minister says
item.description: “The question of graduation doesn’t arise now, because almost all economic indicators are declining,” the finance minister said.

item.title: Climate-resilient, high-yield Aush variety developed through GAU research
item.description: The researchers believe their variety could transform Aush farming, especially in northern Bangladesh.

item.title: Hospital calls father three days after daughter’s death to offer ICU bed
item.description: Fatima Khatun had shown measles symptoms for 15 days. Doctors recommended an ICU bed, but the family spent the day searching without success.

item.title: BGB deploys 16 platoons in Sherpur-3 ahead of by-election
item.description: BGB personnel will serve as both mobile and static forces. They will patrol for five days before polling, on election day itself, and for two days afterwards.

item.title: Measles outbreak most alarming in Bangladesh?
item.description: The WHO has yet to publish comparable country-by-country data. Its statistics show just 6,726 confirmed cases for all of Southeast Asia in January and February. Bangladesh alone recorded 6,476 children with measles symptoms needing medical care from mid-March.

item.title: Authorities seize 47,000 litres of diesel in six districts, 13 detained
item.description: The largest single seizure occurred in Barguna, where the National Security Intelligence (NSI) raided a godown in Patharghata and recovered 38,000 litres of diesel stored in 193 barrels.

item.title: Satellite firm Planet Labs to indefinitely withhold Iran war images
item.description: The restriction expands upon a 14-day delay on imagery of the Middle East ​that Planet Labs imposed last month, a move the firm said was meant to prevent ⁠adversaries from using it to attack the U.S. and its allies.

item.title: Bangladesh Bank shortens hours, sets 3pm transaction cutoff
item.description: Banks will now run from 10 am to 5 pm, with customer transactions permitted until 3 pm, the Bangladesh Bank said in a circular on Saturday.

item.title: Iran leaves door open for peace talks as hunt for missing US pilot continues
item.description: With Iran’s leadership defiant since the start ​of the war, its foreign minister in principle left the door open for holding peace talks with the U.S. via mediation from Pakistan, but gave no sign of Tehran’s willingness to ​bow to Trump’s demands.

item.title: Rajshahi to vaccinate 140,000 children, city administrator says
item.description: Measles prevalence in Rajshahi remained lower than elsewhere in the country, the administrator said, voicing confidence that existing vaccination efforts could contain the situation.

item.title: Stock market trading hours cut by half an hour
item.description: Trading will now run from 10 am to 2 pm, replacing the previous 2:30 pm close, the DSE said on Saturday.

item.title: Five bodies recovered after Keraniganj gaslight factory fire
item.description: Fire crews extinguished the blaze at 4:44 pm, according to a message from the service.

item.title: Fakhrul rejects ‘anti-reform’ claim, says BNP will deliver changes
item.description: He said the claim that the BNP does not want reforms is completely false and is being used to confuse the nation.

item.title: Three more children die with measles symptoms in Rajshahi
item.description: The Rajshahi Medical College Hospital currently has 149 suspected measles cases under treatment in isolation.

item.title: Telegram’s Durov says Russia triggered payment system problem by blocking VPNs
item.description: Friday’s technical problem, which Russia has yet to fully ​explain, sowed chaos for some shoppers, forcing the Moscow metro to allow entry ​without payment through its turnstiles, while a regional zoo had to ask visitors to use cash.

item.title: Emergency measles vaccination starts Sunday: Health Minister
item.description: The campaign will target children aged six months to 59 months, including those who have already received a measles shot.

item.title: UK charges three men with arson over attack on Jewish community ambulances
item.description: Two ⁠are British nationals, while the third is a dual British-Pakistani citizen.

item.title: Ship carrying 34,000 tonnes of diesel from Malaysia arrives at Ctg Port
item.description: The ship arrived at the Kutubdia Channel in the Bay of Bengal at the outer anchorage of the port on Friday night.

item.title: Falling exports steadily sap momentum at Chittagong port
item.description: Until recently, steady growth in export cargo helped sustain overall growth, but the current export downturn now strains that trend.

item.title: Hormuz blockade casts doubt on Saudi fertiliser; Dhaka seeks alternatives
item.description: No deal has been finalised yet, but preliminary estimates suggest new contracts with China, Morocco and the UAE could bring 1.5 million to 1.8 million tonnes of DAP, MOP and TSP.

item.title: Govt targets 3,100MW daily power savings amid Middle East crisis
item.description: Implementing the plan prioritises irrigation for farmland, securing production, storage and proper distribution of fertiliser, and maintaining fuel supplies to industry on a priority basis to sustain output and growth.

item.title: North American farmers pinch pennies on farm machinery as profitless growing season approaches
item.description: Farmers are still spending money, but far less than in other years, ​according to sales data from the Association of Equipment Manufacturers, the organization that represents big players in the North ⁠American industry.

item.title: Trump proposes “historic” defense spending budget, eyes 10% cut to other federal programs
item.description: The request ultimately requires approval by Congress, where disagreement over Trump’s spending decisions recently led to the longest ‌government shutdown in U.S. history.

item.title: Trump’s anger over Iran thrusts NATO into fresh crisis
item.description: In a speech on Wednesday night, Trump criticized U.S. allies but stopped short of condemning NATO, as many experts thought he might.

item.title: Moderate heatwave sweeps Chuadanga, temperature hits 39.7 °C
item.description: The officer-in-charge of the Chuadanga First-Class Weather Observatory added that the heatwave is expected to persist for the next few days.

item.title: No fuel crisis in country as its import, supply normal: Shahe Alam
item.description: The state minister said that some new steps have been taken to save energy, which include closing the market by 6 pm and reducing the expenses of ministers and secretaries by 30 percent.

item.title: Bangladesh, Japan hold fresh talks on HSIA third terminal
item.description: Officials said the meeting focused on operational and financial aspects of the terminal management, as both sides seek to resolve outstanding issues and move towards a mutually acceptable agreement.

item.title: Cooking gas price jumps nearly 29 percent
item.description: While the government has yet to increase prices for fuel oil and electricity despite the global crisis triggered by the conflict in the Middle East, this monthly LPG adjustment is the highest in several years.

item.title: Microsoft to invest $10 billion in Japan for AI and cyber defence expansion
item.description: Microsoft ​will work with domestic firms, including SoftBank and ​Sakura Internet, to expand Japan-based AI computing capacity, allowing ‌companies ⁠and government agencies to keep sensitive data within the country while accessing Microsoft Azure services, it said.

item.title: UN to vote on Hormuz resolution as China opposes authorization of force
item.description: Oil prices have surged since the United States and Israel struck Iran at the end of February, unleashing a conflict ​that has run for more than a month and effectively closed the key shipping artery.

item.title: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing elected president by pro-military parliament
item.description: The transition from top general to civilian president follows a lopsided ​election in December and January that was won in a landslide by an army-backed party and derided ⁠by critics and Western governments as a sham to perpetuate military rule behind a veneer of democracy.

item.title: Police seized 4,500 litres of diesel in Gangni
item.description: Acting on a tip-off, police conducted a raid while the fuel was being illegally unloaded at a fertilizer and pesticide shop owned by a businessman named Ripon in Shahebnagar Bazar.

item.title: Ship carrying 27,000 tons of diesel from Singapore arrives in Chattogram
item.description: Another vessel carrying 34,000 tons of diesel from Malaysia is scheduled to arrive at the port tonight.

item.title: The Canadian GMO mustard wars: Dijon vs canola
item.description: It’s not without risk, however. The GMO plant looks nearly identical to a traditional ⁠mustard plant. Neighboring fields could be contaminated with seeds and pollen carried on the wind or by bees.

item.title: Trump touts gains against Iran but gives no timeline to end war
item.description: Facing a war-wary ‌American public, sliding approval ratings and pressure from some allies to outline his war aims in more precise and consistent terms, Trump said the U.S. had destroyed Iran’s navy and air force, and crippled its ballistic missile and nuclear programs.

item.title: AI machine sorts clothes faster than humans to boost textile recycling in China
item.description: The Fastsort-Textile machine, named one of Time magazine’s Best Inventions of 2025, was created by DataBeyond, a Chinese AI recycling company founded in 2018.

item.title: Rajshahi administrator vows to reshape city
item.description: Mahfuzur Rahman Riton spoke at a meeting in Nagar Bhaban that the corporation’s engineering department had organised.

item.title: UK gathers more than 30 countries to plot ways of reopening the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iranian attacks on commercial ships, and the threat of more, have halted nearly all traffic in the waterway that connects the Persian Gulf to the rest of the globe’s oceans.

item.title: Energy ministry instructs DCs to ensure diesel supply to farmers
item.description: Joint Secretary of the Ministry of Power, Energy and Mineral Resources, Monir Hossain Chowdhury, urged farmers to inform the local administration if they face any difficulties in obtaining diesel.

item.title: Brazilian researchers remix coffee varieties to confront climate challenge
item.description: By introducing genetic material from more rustic coffee species into new hybrids, scientists at the research institute in ​Sao Paulo state aim to create more resistant arabica varieties.

item.title: LPG prices up, sharp rise for 12kg cylinders
item.description: Jalal Ahmed, the chairman of the Bangladesh Energy Regulatory Commission, unveiled the new rates at 3 pm.

item.title: Puja Udjapan Parishad delegation calls on PM
item.description: The delegation met the premier at the conference room of the Cabinet Division in the Bangladesh Secretariat this noon.

item.title: Iran army chief tells commanders to prepare for any attack, state media reports
item.description: “No enemy troops should survive if adversaries attempt a ground operation,” state media quoted ​Hatami as ​saying.

item.title: Oil jumps nearly 7% after Trump says US to keep up attacks on Iran
item.description: The gains followed an earlier fall of ​more than $1 in both benchmarks prior to Trump’s televised speech ⁠to the nation, after having settled lower in the previous session.

item.title: Govt urges public not to be confused about govt office hours change
item.description: The public administration ministry has confirmed that no such notification has been issued.

item.title: Fuel stocks: petrol and octane up slightly, diesel down
item.description: Separately, authorities recovered 372,388 litres of illegally hoarded fuel between March 3 and April 1.

item.title: Govt targets BDT 1.5 trillion bank borrowing in April–June
item.description: Bank executives doubt the banking sector can supply the government with BDT 1.5 trillion in loans over the next three months. They note that most deposit growth already flows into government borrowing, leaving the private sector credit-starved for years.

item.title: Bangladesh conveys solidarity message to UAE amid regional tensions
item.description: In the letter, the prime minister expressed “firm solidarity” with the UAE leadership, government and brotherly people, given the current conflict.

item.title: Bangladesh industrialists urge proactive, multi-pronged measures
item.description: Bonik Barta spoke with several industrialists to determine what policymakers should do. Their discussions yielded eight key recommendations.

item.title: Lower fuel supply leaves Bangladesh Railway with 15–20 days of diesel
item.description: As stocks fall, railway officials are maintaining regular contact with the state-run Bangladesh Petroleum Corporation (BPC) to secure more supplies. They insist, however, that despite lower inventories, trains face no immediate shortage.

item.title: Supreme Court justices skeptical of Trump order to restrict birthright citizenship
item.description: An eventual ruling by the Supreme ⁠Court endorsing the administration’s view ​could affect the legal status of as many as 250,000 babies born each year, according to some estimates, and require the families of millions more to prove the citizenship status of their newborns.

item.title: NASA launches four astronauts on world’s first crewed lunar mission in half a century
item.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said the launch was an opening act for subsequent missions that would include construction of a moon base to support the “enduring presence we’re trying to create on the surface.”

item.title: Trump says Iran war’s core objectives near completion
item.description: Thousands of people have been killed across the Middle East since February 28, when the U.S. and Israel struck Iran, triggering Iranian attacks on Israel, U.S. bases and ​the Gulf states, while opening a new front in Lebanon.

item.title: Commercial banks’ forex reserves drop to $3.80 billion
item.description: With more than a dozen domestic and foreign banks in a precarious financial state, many have been effectively sidelined from the nation’s import-export trade. For this reason, commercial banks’ foreign exchange reserves are so low.

item.title: The battle for trade routes: How global crises are creating new logistics power
item.description: China is building roads and other infrastructure across continents. India is gradually rising, making itself a manufacturing centre and a strategic bridge at the same time. This is not just economic competition. It is a competition over who will shape the future of global trade flows.

item.title: Nepal is seeking global investment in tourism, clean energy, and digital economy
item.description: In this wide-ranging interview, Dr Shrestha discusses the underlying drivers of regional political shifts, the aspirations of Nepal's youth, and the strategic necessity of enhancing trade and energy cooperation between Kathmandu and Dhaka. He also addresses the burgeoning crisis in the Middle East and its immediate repercussions on fuel prices and labour migration in the Himalayas.

item.title: Govt aims for investment-driven economy over debt: Finance Minister
item.description: The minister said the government’s goal is to shift from a debt-based economy to an investment-based one and to attract investment to generate jobs.

item.title: Gold prices rise again by BDT 3,266 per bhori
item.description: So far this year, gold prices have been adjusted 51 times in the domestic market, consisting of 30 increases and 21 reductions.

item.title: PM asks Education Ministry to find ways to ease school-hour city traffic
item.description: Prime Minister’s Additional Press Secretary Atikur Rahman Ruman said possible ways to reduce traffic congestion in the city during school hours and what alternative arrangements could be introduced for this were discussed in the meeting.

item.title: Names of fake freedom fighters to be removed: Minister
item.description: The minister stated that over the last 17 years, more than 100,000 individuals were wrongfully listed and granted various benefits, including allowances.

item.title: Govt debt emerges as fastest-growing sector with around 30 percent surge
item.description: According to the Bangladesh Bank, the government’s outstanding bank debt stood at around BDT 4.34 trillion at the end of January 2025. By the end of January this year, that figure reached BDT 5.63 trillion. This represents an increase of BDT 1.28 trillion in outstanding debt within a year, reflecting a growth of 29.66 percent.

item.title: Thousands of US Army paratroopers arrive in Middle East as buildup intensifies
item.description: The soldiers could be used for several purposes in the ​Iran war, including an attempt to seize Kharg Island, the hub for 90 percent of Iran’s oil exports.

item.title: Giant oil tanker off Dubai hit by Iranian strike after Trump’s latest threats
item.description: Crude oil prices briefly spiked again after the attack on the tanker, which can carry around 2 million barrels of oil worth more than $200 million at current prices.

item.title: Diesel reserves at 128000 tons, octane stocks at 7,940 tons
item.description: According to the official figures, as of today, diesel reserves stand at 128,939 tons, octane at 7,940 tons, petrol at 11,431 tons, and aviation jet fuel at 44,609 tons.

item.title: Chinese Ambassador calls on State Minister for Sports
item.description: Aminul Haque extended an invitation to the Chinese national men’s football team to play a friendly match in Bangladesh and urged the Chinese government to invest in the country’s Sports Institute.

item.title: Gold prices rise by BDT 3,266 per bhori
item.description: This increase marks the 50th gold price adjustment by BAJUS so far this year. Of these, prices were hiked 29 times and lowered on 21 occasions. In contrast, gold prices were adjusted only 17 times during the same period last year.

item.title: Govt needs extra $3 billion for fuel imports by June
item.description: To secure this funding, the government has initiated efforts to obtain loans from several international agencies.

item.title: Myanmar military chief Min Aung Hlaing steps down, eyes presidency
item.description: The move follows a controversial election held amid raging conflict in December and ​January, won by the military-backed Union Solidarity and Development Party but widely derided as a sham by the United Nations and many Western countries.

item.title: Iran war volatility strains trading in world’s biggest markets
item.description: Investors say they have at times struggled to ‌get prices or execute trades over the past four weeks, as market makers fear being stuck with large positions that could quickly become unprofitable.

item.title: PM urges athletes to stay away from politics during careers
item.description: The premier said that sports are no longer just a matter of hobby, entertainment, or physical exercise, but are now recognized as a profession worldwide.

item.title: Jatri Kalyan Samity reports 351 killed in road accidents during Eid rush
item.description: This year, the number of road accidents rose by 8.95 percent, fatalities by 8.26 percent, and injuries by 21.05 percent compared to the previous year.

item.title: Govt announces BDT 100,000 reward for information on fuel hoarders
item.description: Officials believe that specific information will allow the administration to take swift action and simplify legal proceedings against those responsible.

item.title: Fuel supply halted in 8 northern districts as tank lorry workers launch strike
item.description: The workers claimed they are protesting against police harassment on highways and the unreasonable sentencing of drivers, managers, and helpers of the tank lorry.

item.title: Fascist regime committed widespread corruption in name of development: Mirza Fakhrul
item.description: Mirza Fakhrul said they won’t tolerate any corruption and will work, giving the utmost importance to rural roads and communication.

item.title: Home Minister seeks French support for police reform, capacity building
item.description: The minister said that improving the country’s law and order situation is the top priority of the current government, emphasizing ongoing efforts to reform and strengthen law enforcement agencies, including the police.

item.title: Swiss back tougher social media rules for minors, survey finds
item.description: On Wednesday, a Los ​Angeles jury found Meta and Alphabet’s Google negligent for ​designing social media platforms that are harmful to ⁠young people, in a verdict that will serve ​as a bellwether for numerous similar cases.

item.title: Iran warns US over ground attack as regional powers meet in Pakistan
item.description: The foreign ministers of Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt were set to meet in Islamabad ‌to discuss ways to halt the month-long U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, which has killed thousands of people and caused the biggest-ever disruption to global energy supplies.

item.title: Rohingya teen loses leg in landmine blast at Ukhiya border
item.description: The explosion occurred while the teenager was returning to the shore after fishing in the Naf River.

item.title: Bangladesh archery team accorded warm reception after Asia Cup gold
item.description: The youth and sports secretary welcomed the team with floral wreaths. He extended his heartfelt congratulations to the players, coaches, and officials for their outstanding achievement.

item.title: Army Chief General Waker-Uz-Zaman on official visit to US
item.description: During the visit, the Army Chief will receive an honourary motion from the Georgia State Capitol acknowledging the commendable contributions of Bangladeshi peacekeepers in United Nations missions.

item.title: Govt confident in tackling fuel crisis despite volatile ground reality
item.description: Industrialists and business leaders warn that unless the fuel supply is normalized quickly, it will have a direct impact on market prices, production, and the overall economy.

item.title: DAP estimates over BDT 500 billion needed to restore 65 canals in Dhaka
item.description: The landscape has shifted drastically over the last three decades. Unplanned urbanization, encroachment, and pollution have rendered these canals nearly invisible. In many places, they have turned into narrow drains or have been filled in to make way for permanent structures.

item.title: Nepal’s ex-PM Oli arrested over deaths during Gen Z protests
item.description: Oli and his home minister were taken ​into custody one day after rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah was sworn in ​as prime minister, and after a recommendation last week by ⁠a Nepali panel investigating violence during the protests that they should ​be prosecuted for negligence.

item.title: One month into Iran war, only hard choices for Trump
item.description: The central question now, say analysts, is whether Trump is ready to wind down or ramp up what critics have called a war of choice, one that has ignited the worst global energy supply shock in ‌history and spread far beyond the region.

item.title: Gold price rises by BDT 2,157 per bhori
item.description: BAJUS has increased the price of 22-carat gold by BDT 2,157, setting the new rate at BDT 237,012 per bhori. The new prices became effective at 10 am today.

item.title: Rain forecast for all divisions across Bangladesh
item.description: The weather forecast today also indicates that day and night temperatures may fall by 1–2°C nationwide.

item.title: Three more LNG-carrying ships to arrive at Chittagong port
item.description: One of the ships has already entered the country’s territorial waters, with the remaining two scheduled to arrive by Wednesday. The total LNG capacity of the three ships is around 193,000 tonnes.

item.title: Malaysia to reduce foreign workforce at 5%; Thousands of Bangladeshis face uncertainty
item.description: According to the Bangladesh High Commission in Malaysia and various related sources, more than 800,000 Bangladeshis are currently employed in the country. Additionally, several hundred thousand more are estimated to be residing there without valid documentation.

item.title: Japan’s ispace delays NASA-sponsored moon landing to 2030
item.description: The announcement highlights the murky outlook for the ‌venture, as the U.S. revamps space missions with commercial and international partners to send astronauts to the Moon before China does.

item.title: Gold price drops by BDT 6,590 per bhori
item.description: The price adjustment comes in response to a decline in the price of pure (tejabi) gold in the local market. The Bangladesh Jewellers Association (BAJUS) announced that the new rates became effective at 10 am today.

item.title: Govt to buy two LNG cargoes from UK at nearly double the price
item.description: While the current price remains double the standard rate, the approval was granted yesterday at a price slightly lower than the recent peaks.

item.title: SpaceX IPO leaves some private share buyers unsure what they own
item.description: The intense demand for SpaceX shares has led investors to accept unusually complex arrangements, according to 10 investors, industry experts and analysts interviewed by Reuters.

item.title: Revenue deficit exceeds BDT 714 billion in first eight months of FY26
item.description: The NBR set a revenue collection target of around BDT 3.25 trillion for the first eight months of the fiscal year. However, actual collection stood at around BDT 2.54 billion, resulting in a deficit of BDT 714.72 billion.

item.title: Boro rice submerged in Sunamganj haors amid upstream runoff and rain
item.description: Water Development Board (WDB) stated that 98 percent of embankment construction is complete, and grass is currently being planted on the embankments. It added that there is no immediate threat of early floods.

item.title: Mosquito surge grips Dhaka as experts warn dengue cases may climb
item.description: Entomologists say the heavy rains during the recent Eid holiday have now stirred Aedes mosquitoes into action. They warn that dengue cases could rise within two weeks.

item.title: Jet fuel shock spreads globally, Bangladesh sees price jump 80%
item.description: Senior officials at private airlines warn that such a steep rise will force up ticket prices, erode passenger numbers and weaken their ability to compete with foreign carriers at home.

item.title: OpenAI drops AI video tool Sora, startling Disney, sources say
item.description: The move is the first big step by the ChatGPT maker to focus its business on potentially more lucrative areas such as ​coding tools and corporate customers.

item.title: German military satellite plan fuels EU fragmentation fears
item.description: European Union lawmakers told Reuters that Germany’s solo initiative risks undermining attempts to bolster collective defence capabilities as the bloc adapts to the relative decline of the U.S. defence umbrella under President Donald Trump.

item.title: Brazilian city trials new biofuel aimed at replacing diesel entirely
item.description: The city of Passo Fundo is deploying a diesel alternative for its municipal fleet made from feedstocks including soy, animal fats and used oils.

item.title: Making the most of your festival bonus: Lessons from financial literacy
item.description: While festivals are meant for happiness and sharing, an important question arises: should we spend the entire festival bonus at once?

item.title: Middle East War and Global Energy Security: Lessons for Bangladesh
item.description: The ongoing conflict not only threatens immediate fuel supply stability but also raises urgent questions about how Bangladesh should strengthen its long-term energy security strategy in an increasingly uncertain geopolitical environment.

item.title: Rethinking public housing delivery: A global crisis and Bangladesh’s reform opportunity
item.description: The problem is rarely engineering. More often, it is the way housing projects are managed.

item.title: A major milestone for Bangladesh’s cinema industry
item.description: Competing against 11 other features in the Big Screen Competition at the International Film Festival Rotterdam (IFFR), the Bangladeshi film “Master” has earned its place among the world’s most prestigious film festivals.

item.title: Bangladeshi folk film ‘Behula Dorodi’ to screen at international festivals
item.description: Jahidul Islam, the producer, said he aims to establish Bengali cinema as a viable commercial proposition internationally while preserving “dying folk traditions” for a new generation through the film.

item.title: Large-scale deregulation will be BNP government’s biggest programme
item.description: Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury stated that every citizen must have the opportunity to participate in Bangladesh’s economy. To ensure that, he said, it’s necessary to move beyond the prevailing economic trajectory and develop a new model.

item.title: Warner Bros rejects Paramount’s revised offer, sets 7-day deadline for better bid
item.description: Paramount informally broached an even higher share price, $31 a share, Warner Bros said, apparently enticing the board to the table. However, its response to Paramount still makes it clear that Warner Bros prefers the Netflix deal, and the odds of a switch are long.

item.title: Will the T20 World Cup finally witness a 300-run total?
item.description: Such a question no longer sounds absurd, as batters are scoring at supersonic speed. In men’s T20 cricket, there have already been five 300-plus team totals, three of them in international T20s.

item.title: 2nd CA Bangladesh Cup golf tournament ties sport to professional networking
item.description: The tournament was held at the Jolshiri Golf Club, organised by Corporate Tour on behalf of the CA Golfers community.

item.title: Bangladesh reach final after thrashing Nepal
item.description: Earlier in the tournament, Bangladesh had routed Bhutan 12–0 in their opening match before defeating India 2–0 in the second.

item.title: Women seal sixth straight win in T20 World Cup qualifiers
item.description: A total of 12 teams will compete in the tournament. With Bangladesh and the Netherlands securing places from the qualifiers, in total, 10 teams have so far booked their tickets in the tournament.

item.title: Bangladesh bow out after seven-wicket defeat to England
item.description: The defeat ended Bangladesh’s campaign before the semi-finals. Bangladesh will play a formalities match against Zimbabwe on January 31.

item.title: Bangladesh claim inaugural SAFF Women’s Futsal trophy with record win
item.description: They finished top of the table with 16 points from six matches, securing five wins and one draw.

item.title: Bangladeshi short film ‘The Haze’ earns international recognition for original score
item.description: With no spoken dialogue and shot in black and white, sound and music guide the audience through the protagonist’s psychological descent, underscoring the film’s immersive narrative approach.

item.title: “I didn’t want to miss the chance of working on a Humayun Ahmed story”
item.description: Actress Azmeri Haque Badhon is appearing in the film Bonolota Express, based on a story by renowned fiction writer Humayun Ahmed. She has reshaped herself anew for the role. The actress recently spoke to Bonik Barta about her experience, from choosing the character to losing weight and preparing for the part. Interview conducted by Wahidur Rahman.

item.title: “I went through 29 interviews in four months to become vice-chancellor”
item.description: “From application to final interview, I went through 29 interviews. Some of these were quite unusual. In one, I had to give a five-minute interview to a BBC journalist. That was a test. Another was to prepare a presentation on what university financing and the education system would look like over the next five years.”

item.title: “I want to study film in the future”
item.description: Sadia Ayman maintains a balance between her career and personal life. Alongside acting, she hopes to make a positive contribution to the industry as well. Apart from this, she makes time for her family outside of work.

item.title: “Bangladesh is still in a plastic and malleable moment”
item.description: “Things have long seemed unsettled, and that hasn’t changed… I think Bangladesh is still in that plastic, malleable moment. There is reason for hope, but there are warning signs as well.”

item.title: “Providing employment is the most meaningful contribution a company can make”
item.description: “From my perspective, providing employment is the most meaningful contribution a company can make. It gives people dignity, allows them to learn new skills, and fosters independence.”

item.title: “Restricting competition in pharmaceutical industry is another attempt to harm the economy”
item.description: “...if pharmaceutical companies are completely removed from marketing opportunities, there will be no competition among them. Competition, however, drives down drug prices and encourages technological advancements, which improve quality and reduce costs. Competition and marketing are therefore essential components of an open economy.”

item.title: “Chittagong Port now an arena for open competition”
item.description: Rear Admiral SM Moniruzzaman is the Chairman of Chittagong Port Authority. In an interview with Bonik Barta, he discussed a wide range of issues: the port’s operational efficiency, appointment of foreign terminal operators, progress on Bay Terminal and Matarbari projects, measures to ensure transparency, and Chittagong Port’s international reputation.
